# Riz

> Self-hosted AWS Lambda + API Gateway v2 runtime. Run your Lambda functions on your own box, unmodified. Single Rust binary. Drop-in compatibility with the AWS API Gateway v2 HTTP event payload — handlers written for AWS Lambda compile and run on Riz with zero changes.

Riz is an open-source self-hosted runtime that emulates AWS Lambda + API Gateway v2. You point your `riz.toml` at your handler files; Riz spawns one isolated child-process pool per function, dispatches HTTP requests using the canonical AWS API Gateway v2 HTTP payload shape (via the `aws_lambda_events` crate), and exposes a ratatui terminal dashboard with real percentile latency tracking (P50/P75/P90/P95/P99 over a 5-minute rolling window), per-route + per-function Prometheus metrics, hot-reload of config, zero-downtime hot-swap deploys from S3, and a fully MCP-spec-compliant JSON-RPC server that turns every function into an LLM-callable tool.

Function-centric config matches AWS exactly: one `[function.<name>]` block per function, one process pool, N declared routes per function. AWS-style path syntax (`{id}`, `{proxy+}`), AWS-style handler resolution (`handler = "index.handler"` splits into file + export name), AWS-style `$default` catch-all route key, AWS-style `stage` and `stage_variables`, AWS-style `integration_timeout_ms` separate from handler `timeout_ms`. Handlers receive the event in the exact shape AWS sends — same crate, same field names, same serde JSON.

## Author

- [Chris Rizzuto](https://linkedin.com/in/ship247) — chris@riz.dev — 20 years engineering leadership at Meta, Google, Microsoft, Oracle.

## Project

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/24X7/riz): source, issues, releases
- [Releases](https://github.com/24X7/riz/releases): prebuilt binaries for Linux and macOS (x86_64 + aarch64)
- [Install script](https://riz.dev/install): one-line install — `curl -fsSL https://riz.dev/install | sh`

## What works today (v0.1)

- **AWS API Gateway v2 HTTP payload** — exact `aws_lambda_events::apigw::ApiGatewayV2httpRequest`/`Response` types, including `cookies`, `queryStringParameters`, `multiValueHeaders`, full `requestContext` with `time`, `timeEpoch`, `userAgent`, `stage`, `accountId`, etc.
- **Function-centric config** — `[function.<name>]` blocks with optional `[[function.<name>.routes]]`, mirroring AWS Lambda's "one function = one execution environment" model
- **Bun runtime** (TypeScript / JavaScript)
- **Node.js runtime** — `runtime = "node"`, `handler = "index.handler"` resolves to file `index.mjs` + export `handler`. The #1 production Lambda runtime; existing Node Lambda code drops in unmodified. Subprocess adapter (ESM) embedded in the binary.
- **Python runtime** — `runtime = "python"`, `handler = "app.lambda_handler"` resolves to file `app.py` + attribute `lambda_handler`. Subprocess adapter embedded in the binary.
- **Rust runtime** — `runtime = "rust"`, `handler = "./target/release/my-handler"` invokes the pre-compiled binary speaking line-delimited JSON stdin/stdout. Reference crate `riz-rust-runtime` provides the boilerplate.
- **AWS-style handler syntax** — `handler = "index.handler"` resolves to file `index.ts` + export `handler`; explicit path form also supported
- **AWS path patterns** — `{name}` single-segment captures, `{proxy+}` greedy multi-segment captures, `$default` HTTP catch-all
- **`ANY` method** — matches all HTTP verbs (AWS API GW v2 semantic)
- **Separate `timeout_ms` and `integration_timeout_ms`** — handler-side vs gateway-side limits, matching AWS Lambda + API Gateway behavior
- **`stage_variables`** per function, surfaced as `event.stageVariables`
- **Auth-aware response cache** — automatically bypassed when `Authorization` or `Cookie` headers are present
- **Hot-reload** of `riz.toml` with diff-aware function add/remove/replace
- **Hot-swap deploys** from S3 that drain in-flight invocations before killing old processes
- **Per-function process isolation** with semaphore-bounded concurrency, automatic respawn on crash/timeout, liveness watcher
- **Terminal dashboard** (ratatui) — real nearest-rank P50/P75/P90/P95/P99 over a 5-minute rolling window, plus host process stats and system endpoint visibility
- **Prometheus metrics** at `/_riz/metrics` — `riz_invocations_total`, `riz_errors_total`, `riz_latency_ms` (summary), `riz_cold_starts_total`, `riz_function_healthy`, `riz_uptime_seconds`
- **MCP server** at `/_riz/mcp` — JSON-RPC 2.0, defaults to spec **2025-11-25** (current stable). Negotiates legacy 2024-11-05 / 2025-03-26 / 2025-06-18 too — older clients get their requested version echoed on `initialize`. Methods: `initialize`, `notifications/initialized`, `ping`, `tools/list`, `tools/call`, `resources/list`, `resources/templates/list`, `prompts/list`. JSON-RPC batching is still accepted (removed in 2025-06-18 but kept for legacy-client compat). Every user function in `riz.toml` automatically becomes an MCP tool — no SDK, no wrappers.
- **Health endpoints** — `/health` (liveness 200), `/ready` (per-pool readiness), `/_riz/health` (rich per-function status)
- **30-second graceful drain** on SIGTERM/SIGINT (hard cap enforced)
- **Single Rust binary** with embedded Bun and Python adapters (no install dance)
- **OpenTelemetry export** — hand-rolled OTLP/HTTP-JSON span exporter, one pipeline fanning out to Datadog and CloudWatch/X-Ray, alongside Prometheus
- **WebSocket APIs** — `$connect` / `$disconnect` / `$default` lifecycle events shaped as `ApiGatewayWebsocketProxyRequest`. Built-in `@connections` management API at `/_riz/connections/{id}` (GET / POST / DELETE) for handlers to push messages back to connected clients. **Bun, Python, and Rust** handlers all supported and end-to-end tested (see `tests/websocket_integration.rs`, `tests/middleware_ws_python.rs`, `tests/middleware_ws_rust.rs`).
- **Lambda authorizers** — REQUEST type (calls a user-declared function) and JWT type (validates against a JWKS URL). Responses cached by source IP + Authorization header hash for configurable TTL (default 5 min). `requestContext.authorizer` populated on success.
- **CORS auto-preflight** — `[cors]` config block (global + per-function override). OPTIONS requests return 204 with `Access-Control-Allow-*` headers; non-OPTIONS requests get origin echoed when in allowlist.
- **Bearer-token auth on `/_riz/*`** — set `RIZ_AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN` or `[auth] bearer_token` in `riz.toml`. `/_riz/health` always open (liveness probes). Constant-time token comparison.
- **Real Lambda context** — `getRemainingTimeInMillis()` uses the actual per-request deadline, `functionName` from `riz.toml`, `invokedFunctionArn` is a synthetic `arn:riz:lambda:local:...` ARN, `awsRequestId` matches `event.requestContext.requestId`.
- **On-box safety profile (always-on)** — every spawned child gets `RLIMIT_CORE=0` (no core-dump disk fill), `RLIMIT_NOFILE=4096` (FD-leak cap), `RLIMIT_FSIZE=100MiB` (single-write cap). Linux only: `PR_SET_PDEATHSIG(SIGKILL)` (kernel SIGKILLs orphan children if riz daemon dies), `PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS` (privilege escalation downgrade), `RLIMIT_NPROC=256` (fork-bomb cap). Applied in `pre_exec` after fork, before execve.
- **Opt-in per-function safety caps** — `memory_mb` field on `[function.<name>]` maps to `RLIMIT_AS` (mirrors AWS Lambda's `MemorySize`). `cpu_time_secs` maps to `RLIMIT_CPU` (kills runaway loops via SIGXCPU then SIGKILL). `allowed_paths = ["./handler", "/tmp"]` enforces a Linux Landlock filesystem allowlist (kernel 5.13+).
- **WebSocket `@connections` list endpoint** — `GET /_riz/connections` returns a JSON array of `{connectionId, function, connectedAgeSecs}` for all live WS connections. Operator tool for inspecting state.
- **`riz init <template>` scaffolding** — 7 built-in templates: `typescript-http`, `python-http`, `rust-http`, `nodejs-http`, `typescript-websocket`, `python-websocket`, `rust-websocket`. Each scaffolds a working handler + `riz.toml` + README. Embedded in the binary via `include_str!`; no network access required for scaffolding.
- **Handler-source hot-reload** — edit `index.ts` / `main.py` / `src/main.rs`, save, the next request hits the new code. Recursive `notify` watcher on each function's handler directory, debounced 200ms, falls through to `process_manager.hot_swap()` per affected function.
- **Production reliability** — all 20 entries in `docs/production-bugs.md` resolved with code-line references + regression-gate test names. `tests/landing_page_contract.rs` checks the landing-page `riz.toml` snippet parses + validates against the real config code.

## Coming (v0.2 and beyond)

- **Capability-sandboxed WebAssembly (WASI) handlers** — the differentiator no Lambda emulator currently ships. WASM modules wrapped in a host process that grants filesystem / network / clock capabilities explicitly via `riz.toml`. Same line-delimited JSON protocol as Bun/Python/Rust adapters; the WASM runtime (wasmtime/wasmer) enforces the capability boundary. Sub-millisecond cold start. Targets multi-tenant SaaS workloads and untrusted-MCP-tools use cases where running arbitrary code with OS-level access is unacceptable.
- **Go support** — Go binaries are static, so they speak the existing Rust-adapter line-delimited JSON protocol without a new runtime kernel. Scope: a `riz-go-runtime` Go module that wraps the stdin/stdout loop + `riz init go-http` / `go-websocket` templates + example handlers + docs.
- **Java / JVM runtime adapter** — JNI-bridged process speaking the same JSON protocol.
- **MCP per-route tool schemas** — typed input shapes synthesized from path + query parameters, replacing the generic-envelope schema.
- **MCP OAuth 2.1 path** — RFC 8707 Resource Indicators, Protected Resource Metadata (RFC 9728). Bearer-token path remains the default.
- **OpenTelemetry exporter** — W3C Trace Context propagation in `_meta` fields. X-Ray header propagation comes free.
- **Non-HTTP event sources** (SQS, SNS, S3, EventBridge, scheduled) — requires polling/push-source adapters; different product surface from HTTP/WS.

## Requirements

- **Runtime dependency**: Bun must be installed on PATH (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`) — Riz spawns Bun processes to execute TypeScript/JavaScript handlers
- **Linux or macOS** (x86_64 or aarch64). Windows not supported.
